name: tdc-caco2

env_axis.name: scaffold
env_axis.shift_mechanism: Bemis-Murcko scaffold-based split; tests novel-scaffold generalization

ood_protocol.name: tdc-caco2-scaffold
ood_protocol.ref: Huang et al. 2021 Nature Chemical Biology; Wang et al. (original Caco-2 data)

facts: Caco-2 cell permeability measurements (ADMET oral absorption proxy); ~900 compounds; canonical scaffold split.
hypotheses: TDC-Caco2's scaffold-split and a DrugOOD-equivalent scaffold-split produce different shifts on overlapping data rather than agreeing.
Caco-2 is an outlier rather than a representative ADMET task (small-N, high literature variance).
intuitions: Caco-2 cross-pipeline benchmarks likely exist for 2024-25.
Foundation-molecular-model results on TDC ADMET probably already exist.
unknowns: N≈900 is borderline for statistical resolution of the scaffold shift, so whether the observed method ranking is signal or noise cannot be determined at this scale; this is a limit of data size, not of the shift itself.
epistemic_state.boundary: Very small; useful as a canonical-but-noisy ADMET probe, not for robust shift claims.

representational_commitments: Caco-2 cell line as proxy for oral absorption

curation_provenance.ref: Huang et al. 2021 Nature Chemical Biology
